notes: Plaintiff opening work notes, 2026-06-30T04:51:49Z. Opportunity openings:plaintiff; final action allowed record_opening_statement only, no evidence admission. Record scan completed with get_case/list_evidence; five juror-visible case-packet text files only, no submitted_evidence/exhibits/arguments. Used stat_evidence and read_evidence_range for all packet files. ev_90fa market-page supplies market rule: qualifying note/message must be credibly reported as written by Epstein and intended as suicide note/final message/equivalent; public availability by listed date; resolution source consensus credible reporting. ev_e631 primary-evidence contains Reuters/NPR/BBC/Guardian excerpts: federal judge released/unsealed a document described as a suicide note purported/alleged to have been written by Epstein; report excerpts quote/describe note language about choosing one's time to say goodbye; all note authenticity limits. ev_b3b official-source-record identifies SDNY Judge Karas/Tartaglione source path and says packet lacks official court PDF/order/note image; reporting says judge unsealed but did not authenticate or assess chain. ev_d6c secondary-reporting identifies NYT and other sources but lacks full NYT capture. ev_2680 unresolved gaps identifies missing SDNY docket PDF/order/note image, full NYT capture, consensus/authentication, intent/authorship issues. Native investigation before opening: web searches for Reuters May 6 2026, SDNY Tartaglione Epstein note Karas PDF, exact note phrases. Found and opened official SDNY PDF at https://www.nysd.uscourts.gov/sites/default/files/2026-05/16cr832%20-%20Tartaglione%20Order%20-%2026.5.6.pdf. Official order dated May 6, 2026 grants Times request as to a 'suicide note purportedly authored by Jeffrey Epstein,' directs clerk to docket copy of material placed in vault May 20, 2021 and unseal original material; does not authenticate authorship or intent. Search also found Reuters-syndicated, PBS, Al Jazeera, Fox, Axios, Guardian, UPI, Business Insider corroborating public release before May 8 and highlighting purported/alleged/unverified status. Could not locate official docketed note image as NYSD standalone PDF during opening; next phase should search for docketed note/vault material, likely after order. Source map: decisive elements are public availability by May 8, note/message, credible reporting to have been written by Epstein, and suicide/final-message/equivalent intent. Best primary targets next phase: SDNY order PDF, official docketed note image/PDF; confirmations Reuters/NPR/BBC/Guardian/NYT; adverse checks are no authentication, uncertain custody via Tartaglione, unsigned/undated note, ambiguity of meaning. Opening theory: preponderance is met by credible public reporting and public availability, not by conclusive handwriting proof; be candid about custody/authentication gaps and say they go to weight, not threshold.

Defendant opening work notes. Objective: opening statement on proposition that by May 8 2026 11:59 PM ET a message/note credibly reported to have been written by Jeffrey Epstein and intended as suicide note/final message/equivalent had been public. Opportunity openings:defendant; final act record_opening_statement only; no evidence admission allowed.

Record scan: get_case and list_evidence show five case-packet files only plus plaintiff opening; no party-submitted evidence. Read all five packet files with read_evidence_range. ev_90fa205907f7_market-page preserves rule: qualifying note must be credibly reported written by Epstein and intended as suicide note/final message/equivalent; public availability by any means; resolution source consensus of credible reporting; May 8 market question despite grouped event May 31 API end date. ev_e631aed74f36_primary-evidence is source map, not closed exhibit package; Reuters/NPR/BBC/Guardian excerpts describe release/unsealing before May 8 but repeatedly hedge authorship and state lack of authentication/verification. NPR excerpt says DOJ told NPR the note had not yet been authenticated and this was first time DOJ saw it. BBC excerpt says only seven lines, leaving uncertainty over meaning. ev_b3b39b867ad2_official-source-record says public record indicates Judge Kenneth M. Karas released one-page note in Tartaglione case after NYT request, but local court PDF/order/note image not included; reporting says judge did not vouch authenticity or chain. ev_d6c0d4659002_secondary-reporting has NYT leads but no full NYT capture. ev_2680dc66be4e_unresolved-record-gaps explicitly identifies missing SDNY docket/order/note image, full NYT capture, unresolved credible consensus, and unresolved intent.

Native investigation: web searches for Epstein/Tartaglione/Karas/released note and official court file found likely official order PDF at https://www.nysd.uscourts.gov/sites/default/files/2026-05/16cr832%20-%20Tartaglione%20Order%20-%2026.5.6.pdf. Web-opened PDF lines indicate SDNY Case No. 16-CR-832 (KMK), Judge Karas, order dated May 6 2026 granting NYT request as to alleged suicide note purportedly authored by Epstein and directing Clerk to docket/unseal material placed in vault May 20 2021. This is a source target for later evidence, not admitted proof for opening. Search also found AP/PBS/CBS leads emphasizing lack of authentication/DOJ first seeing it and Tartaglione source-chain issues, for later evidence.

Theory: concede current packet supports that public reporting existed before May 8 describing a purported/alleged note. Defense should focus that proposition requires credible reporting of both authorship by Epstein and suicide/final-message intent. Current record itself embeds material doubts: no official authentication, judge did not assess chain or authorship, DOJ first seeing it, source appears Tartaglione/counsel in Tartaglione case rather than Epstein death investigation, and short text meaning is uncertain. Missing primary documents matter to weight. Plaintiff's best answer is that 'credibly reported' accepts hedging; response is that credible reporting of a controversy is not the same as credible reporting that both qualifying elements are more likely than not satisfied.

Durable notes written to /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md with search log and next evidence targets: SDNY order PDF, docketed note image/material, full Reuters/AP/NPR/BBC/Guardian captures, NYT accessible capture, later authentication/corrections.

Plaintiff merits work notes, 2026-06-30T04:59Z. Current opportunity arguments:plaintiff; submit_evidence and submit_argument allowed. Record scan after defendant opening: get_case/list_evidence showed no new defendant evidence, only five case-packet files and the two openings. Defendant theory is authorship/intent not proven because sources hedge and lack authentication/chain. Evidence plan: preserve official SDNY order and strongest reporting sources, with adverse caveats. Downloaded official SDNY order from https://www.nysd.uscourts.gov/sites/default/files/2026-05/16cr832%20-%20Tartaglione%20Order%20-%2026.5.6.pdf to /aar-codex/sources/sdny-order.pdf, size 128,992, sha256 7225441cb802084e2b9485ebc2c6d246fbfa08e109fe650cb14f445dc4745ca0. pdftotext absent. Attempted chunked binary upload, began upl_5eb198424cc0feb4d706be65, but shell output for base64 chunk was truncated in tool output, so did not write/commit binary to avoid corruption; submitted focused official-order text capture instead, ev_3ed1398d0c91..., official order describing Times request as a suicide note purportedly authored by Jeffrey Epstein and ordering docket/unseal May 6. Downloaded Reuters/KFGO article https://kfgo.com/2026/05/06/new-york-judge-releases-purported-epstein-suicide-note/ to /aar-codex/sources/kfgo.html, size 84,199, sha256 4d9208464749fe1318d1d43505c3da074f5778beaebc328282435cb73dcf4b21; submitted text capture ev_6b611c640731..., confirming May 6 Reuters report, federal judge released document described as suicide note purportedly written by Epstein, note text, Tartaglione source, explicit caveat judge did not vouch authenticity or chain. Downloaded NPR https://www.npr.org/2026/05/07/nx-s1-5814808/jeffrey-epstein-suicide-note size 110,712 sha256 d2748f008b75082bb37a08d10fd6b797cde386ad48ab58427a9622584bcada48; submitted ev_d9d8f4d4776e..., confirming public release, unsealed goodbye note, NPR non-verification, DOJ not authenticated/first time seeing, Barket no handwriting analysis but comfortable from circumstances/similar writing. Downloaded BBC https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cd7pd0rqwreo size 297,970 sha256 fd96ddd6da9127a93e1fc36173c526363481a9dda14477b74b4bc33dd332749a; full HTML too large/noisy so submitted targeted extraction ev_eb7c336c85a2..., corroborating public release, note text, non-verification, DOJ had not seen caveat, May 2021 letter/original document. Post-submission list_evidence/stat_evidence confirms all four submitted evidence items juror-visible/text_readable. Analysis: no conclusive authentication, but proposition requires credible reporting, not court/DOJ authentication. Official order anchors actual court unsealing before deadline; Reuters/NPR/BBC are credible independent outlets reporting public release and the note's suicide/final-message character. Intent supported by reported text choosing one's time to say goodbye, apparent-suicide-attempt context, and consistent public characterization. Weight caveats should be conceded: no court authentication, uncertain chain through Tartaglione, no real handwriting analysis, DOJ/NPR/BBC non-verification. Remaining gap: official docketed note image not captured as standalone official artifact.

Defendant merits work notes. Rechecked record at arguments:defendant: plaintiff added four exhibits. Read ev_3ed1398d0c91 official SDNY order capture; ev_6b611c640731 Reuters/KFGO; ev_d9d8f4d4776e NPR; ev_eb7c336c85a2 BBC. New evidence does prove public unsealing/reporting before May 8. It remains double-edged: SDNY order is a text capture and says access granted for note 'purportedly authored' but does not authenticate handwriting, chain, or intent; Reuters reports judge did not vouch authenticity or assess chain; NPR says it did not independently verify, DOJ had not authenticated and was first seeing it, Barket did no real handwriting analysis, and DOJ disclaimed knowledge of Tartaglione narrative accuracy; BBC says note not verified by BBC and DOJ had not seen it previously.

Native investigation/source check: opened AP article https://apnews.com/article/jeffrey-epstein-suicide-note-5297f49736e625a06d0db3c7b737b997. It adds defense-relevant context: note became public not from DOJ Epstein records but unrelated Tartaglione case; government said it never had the note; DOJ quote says note not authenticated and first time DOJ seeing it; Tartaglione gave note to lawyers to protect himself from claims he harmed Epstein; Tartaglione was later convicted of murders; jail records say Epstein told a guard Tartaglione attacked him, then recanted; if DOJ lawyers saw note in 2021 proceedings they were barred from disclosing it. Submitted targeted AP capture as defense evidence, accepted as ev_4f315084b1b6_submitted-evidence-05-defendant-4f315084b1b6.

Merits theory: Do not dispute public availability/reporting by May 7. Argue plaintiff still has not met preponderance on qualifying elements. The rule asks for a note credibly reported to have been written by Epstein and intended as suicide note/final message/equivalent. Repeated 'purported/alleged' framing plus express non-authentication is credible reporting that a claim exists, not credible reporting that authorship is likely true. Intent is plausible from goodbye language, but note is brief, hard to decipher, tied to July 23 suspected attempt rather than final death, and chain comes from self-interested Tartaglione. Missing admitted evidence: actual court note image, handwriting expert, underlying DOJ document, NYT full petition/source file, court finding on authorship or intent. Offer AP as DX-1 and discuss plaintiff exhibits as adverse/corroborating but caveated sources.

Plaintiff rebuttal work notes, 2026-06-30T05:03Z. Opportunity rebuttals:plaintiff; final actions submit_rebuttal or pass; evidence admission allowed but not needed after targeted review. Record scan: get_case/list_evidence showed defendant submitted one new evidence item, ev_4f315084b1b6... AP report capture, plus defense source-chain/gap ledger. Used stat_evidence and read_evidence_range for ev_4f315084b1b6. Exact AP points: headline says 'Epstein cellmate says he found a suicide note. Justice Department says it's seeing it for first time.' AP image caption says document was released Thursday May 7 2026 by SDNY and shows a note Epstein's former cellmate said he found after Epstein's reported suicide attempt in July 2019. AP reports note was made public as part of unrelated case and DOJ explanation was that it never had the note; DOJ quote says note not yet authenticated and first time DOJ seeing it. AP reports Tartaglione said he found handwritten note in a book after Epstein was found with bedsheet around neck and gave it to lawyers to protect himself; AP reports note in federal court vault since 2021 and became part of attorney-client privileged proceedings; short note hard to decipher and not authenticated; DOJ did not object to release. Rebuttal theory: defense concedes public release before deadline; AP reinforces it. Defense's 'credible reporting of controversy only' rewrites rule too narrowly. Proposition says credibly reported to have been written, not authenticated. Existing record is official court order + Reuters/NPR/BBC/AP, all identifying a real court-released note and consistently describing it as purported/alleged suicide/goodbye note attributed to Epstein. AP source-chain caveats affect weight but also confirm court-release path, court vault since 2021, and suicide-note characterization. Intent is supported by text choosing one's time to say goodbye plus July 2019 suspected suicide-attempt setting and later suicide. No new evidence submitted in rebuttal because opponent's own DX plus existing PX answer strongest defense points.

Defendant surrebuttal work notes. Rechecked live case and list_evidence at surrebuttals:defendant. Plaintiff filed rebuttal but no new evidence was added after defense AP evidence. Evidence list unchanged: case packet plus plaintiff SDNY/Reuters/NPR/BBC captures and defendant AP capture.

Plaintiff rebuttal says defense concedes public unsealing/reporting; argues AP reinforces public availability, court provenance, and suicide-note characterization; says caveats are to certainty only, not to credible reporting; asserts 'purportedly' and 'credibly reported' make uncertainty expected.

Surrebuttal plan: do not repeat full merits. Concede DX-1 confirms public release; that is not disputed. Reframe the remaining issue: whether credible reporting establishes authorship and intent, not merely the existence of a claim. The rebuttal counts multiple outlets but they all depend on the same source chain: Tartaglione/counsel/court-vault/unsealing. None supplies independent authentication; court order is access ruling, not authorship finding; DOJ had not had note; NPR/BBC did not verify; Barket did no real handwriting analysis. The phrase 'purportedly' should not be transformed from warning label into affirmative proof. No new evidence necessary; rely on existing DX-1 and plaintiff exhibits.

Plaintiff closing work notes, 2026-06-30T05:06Z. Opportunity closings:plaintiff; final action deliver_closing_statement; no evidence admission. Record scan with get_case/list_evidence shows final visible record: five case-packet files; plaintiff submitted official SDNY order capture ev_3ed1398d0c91, Reuters/KFGO ev_6b611c640731, NPR ev_d9d8f4d4776e, BBC ev_eb7c336c85a2; defendant submitted AP ev_4f315084b1b6; plaintiff rebuttal and defendant surrebuttal. Defendant surrebuttal concedes public availability/public characterization, but argues 'credibly reported' requires more than repeated hedged reports because sources are not independent authenticators and trace to same source chain. Closing theory: burden is preponderance under exact proposition: a message/note credibly reported to have been written by Epstein and intended as suicide/final/equivalent was public by May 8. Public availability is conceded and proven by official order and AP. Credible reporting is proven by Reuters, NPR, BBC, AP, and packet Guardian lead, all before May 8; they are credible because they report caveats, not because they overclaim. Defense's 'not independent authenticators' point rewrites the market to require authentication; 'reported to have been written' accepts attributed reporting with caveats. Intent is supported by the note's reported text and context, not mere label. Candid caveat: record lacks standalone official note image and conclusive handwriting/chain; under this proposition those are weight limits, not defeating gaps.

Defendant closing work notes. At closings:defendant, ran get_case and list_evidence. No new evidence since surrebuttal. Plaintiff closing repeats that the rule requires credible reporting not authentication; says public availability established by SDNY order/AP; claims Reuters/NPR/BBC/AP consensus; treats caveats as non-dispositive; argues intent from goodbye text plus suicide-attempt/death context.

Closing plan: concise synthesis. Concede public availability and plausible plaintiff reading. Argue plaintiff still fails exact proposition by preponderance because 'credibly reported' modifies authorship and intent; cannot erase the qualifiers 'written by Epstein' and 'intended as suicide note/final message/equivalent.' Evidence weight: PX-1 is access order, not authorship/intent finding. Reuters/NPR/BBC all caveat authenticity/verification; NPR says DOJ first saw it/not authenticated and Barket no real handwriting analysis. DX-1 AP shows DOJ never had it, Tartaglione/counsel unrelated-case source chain, Tartaglione self-protection, and Epstein accusation/recantation context. Multiple outlets repeat same attributed claim, not independent authentication. Intent plausible but record lacks full note image, handwriting expert, chain-of-custody proof, official finding, or complete context. Ask council to reject proposition because public controversy/reporting is not enough for preponderance on qualifying note.
